summary: The texts are one half of the case; the dirt is the other. Across the lands of the story, archaeologists keep pulling the same names and the same events out of the ground — kings, officials, sieges and tunnels the texts had already named, sometimes centuries before the spade confirmed them. This is not proof of every line. It is something quieter and harder to dismiss: again and again, when we can check the ancient world's own account against the physical record, the account holds. Tap any find to see what it confirms.
